revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称 = 丹生川神社 |画像 = |所在地 = [[岐阜県]][[高山市]]丹生川町坊方2373 |位置 = |祭神 = [[カグツチ|火結大神]] |社格 = [[金幣社|銀幣社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''丹生川神社'''（にゅうかわじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]丹生川町（旧・[[大野郡]][[丹生川村]]）に鎮座する[[神社]]…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 丹生川神社 |画像 = |所在地 = [[岐阜県]][[高山市]]丹生川町坊方2373 |位置 = |祭神 = [[カグツチ|火結大神]] |社格 = [[金幣社|銀幣社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''丹生川神社'''（にゅうかわじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]丹生川町（旧・[[大野郡]][[丹生川村]]）に鎮座する[[神社]]。
神社名は[[大野郡]][[丹生川村]]に因む名であり、[[奈良県]]の[[丹生川上神社]]、[[丹生川上神社上社]]、[[丹生川上神社下社]]とは直接の関係は無い。
== 概要 == 創建時期は不詳。かつては「八相の森」「八面荒神宮」と称していた{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}}。
[[1868年]]（明治元年）に八竈神社に改称{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}}。[[1871年]]（明治4年）に村社となる。
[[1907年]]（明治40年）、熊野神社及び貴船神社を合祀。同時に八竈神社から丹生川神社への改称を岐阜県に願い出、翌年許可を得る{{Sfn|丹生川村史|2000|p=436}}。[[1913年]]（大正2年）に熊野神社を合祀。[[1914年]]（大正3年）に'''丹生川神社'''に改称する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。
その後、昭和初期にかけて丹生川村内の神社（神明神社、熊野神社、諏訪神社、美女島神社など）を合祀する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。相次ぐ合祀により境内が手狭となったため、[[1952年]]（昭和27年）に境内の拡張と整備を行い、本殿などを新築{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。同年、白山神社を合祀。
== 主祭神 == * [[カグツチ|火結大神]]
== 合祀{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}} == * [[かまど神|奥津日子大神]] * [[かまど神|奥津日女大神]] * 櫛御気大神 * 速玉男神 * 闇霧神 * [[天照大神|天照皇大神]] * [[大国主|大穴牟遅神]] * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]] * [[スサノオ|建速須佐男神]] * [[タケミナカタ|建御名方神]] * [[八坂刀売神]] * [[イチキシマヒメ|市杵嶋姫神]] * [[イザナギ|伊邪那岐神]] * [[イザナミ|伊邪那美神]] * [[菊理媛神]]
== 境外末社 == * [[招魂社]] : 旧・熊野神社（町方、1913年合祀）跡地{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}
== [[御旅所]] == * 町方（町方の旧・熊野神社（1913年合祀）跡地）と坊方（坊方の旧・熊野神社（1907年合祀）跡地）の2個所がある{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=土田吉左衛門 |title=飛騨の神社 |publisher=飛騨神職会 |date=1987-04 |id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000001983469}} |ref={{SfnRef|土田|1987}}}} * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=丹生川村史編集委員会 |title=丹生川村史 通史編 二 |publisher丹生川村 |date=2000 |ref={{SfnRef|丹生川村史|2000}}}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2145 丹生川神社] - 岐阜県神社庁
{{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:にゆうかわしんしや}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:高山市の歴史]] [[Category:高山市の建築物]]

{{神社 |名称 = 丹生川神社 |画像 = |所在地 = [[岐阜県]][[高山市]]丹生川町坊方2373 |位置 = |祭神 = [[カグツチ|火結大神]] |社格 = [[金幣社|銀幣社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''丹生川神社'''（にゅうかわじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]丹生川町（旧・[[大野郡]][[丹生川村]]）に鎮座する[[神社]]。
神社名は[[大野郡]][[丹生川村]]に因む名であり、[[奈良県]]の[[丹生川上神社]]、[[丹生川上神社上社]]、[[丹生川上神社下社]]とは直接の関係は無い。
== 概要 == 創建時期は不詳。かつては「八相の森」「八面荒神宮」と称していた{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}}。
[[1868年]]（明治元年）に八竈神社に改称{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}}。[[1871年]]（明治4年）に村社となる。
[[1907年]]（明治40年）、熊野神社及び貴船神社を合祀。同時に八竈神社から丹生川神社への改称を岐阜県に願い出、翌年許可を得る{{Sfn|丹生川村史|2000|p=436}}。[[1913年]]（大正2年）に熊野神社を合祀。[[1914年]]（大正3年）に'''丹生川神社'''に改称する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。
その後、昭和初期にかけて丹生川村内の神社（神明神社、熊野神社、諏訪神社、美女島神社など）を合祀する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。相次ぐ合祀により境内が手狭となったため、[[1952年]]（昭和27年）に境内の拡張と整備を行い、本殿などを新築{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。同年、白山神社を合祀。
== 主祭神 == * [[カグツチ|火結大神]]
== 合祀{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}} == * [[かまど神|奥津日子大神]] * [[かまど神|奥津日女大神]] * 櫛御気大神 * 速玉男神 * 闇霧神 * [[天照大神|天照皇大神]] * [[大国主|大穴牟遅神]] * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]] * [[スサノオ|建速須佐男神]] * [[タケミナカタ|建御名方神]] * [[八坂刀売神]] * [[イチキシマヒメ|市杵嶋姫神]] * [[イザナギ|伊邪那岐神]] * [[イザナミ|伊邪那美神]] * [[菊理媛神]]
== 境外末社 == * [[招魂社]] : 旧・熊野神社（町方、1913年合祀）跡地{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}
== [[御旅所]] == * 町方（町方の旧・熊野神社（1913年合祀）跡地）と坊方（坊方の旧・熊野神社（1907年合祀）跡地）の2個所がある{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。
== 天然記念物 == * 丹生川神社の大杉（高山市指定天然記念物）<ref>{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=https://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000994/1004127.html |title=天然記念物一覧 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2025-05-02}}</ref>

== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=土田吉左衛門 |title=飛騨の神社 |publisher=飛騨神職会 |date=1987-04 |id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000001983469}} |ref={{SfnRef|土田|1987}}}} * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=丹生川村史編集委員会 |title=丹生川村史 通史編 二 |publisher丹生川村 |date=2000 |ref={{SfnRef|丹生川村史|2000}}}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2145 丹生川神社] - 岐阜県神社庁
{{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:にゆうかわしんしや}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:高山市の歴史]] [[Category:高山市の建築物]]

{{神社 |名称 = 丹生川神社 |画像 = |所在地 = [[岐阜県]][[高山市]]丹生川町坊方2373 |位置 = |祭神 = [[カグツチ|火結大神]] |社格 = [[金幣社|銀幣社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''丹生川神社'''（にゅうかわじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]丹生川町（旧・[[大野郡]][[丹生川村]]）に鎮座する[[神社]]。

[[1868年]]（明治元年）に八竈神社に改称{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}}。[[1871年]]（明治4年）に村社となる。
[[1907年]]（明治40年）、熊野神社及び貴船神社を合祀。同時に八竈神社から丹生川神社への改称を岐阜県に願い出、翌年許可を得る{{Sfn|丹生川村史|2000|p=436}}。[[1913年]]（大正2年）に熊野神社を合祀。[[1914年]]（大正3年）に'''丹生川神社'''に改称する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。
その後、昭和初期にかけて丹生川村内の神社（神明神社、熊野神社、諏訪神社、美女島神社など）を合祀する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。相次ぐ合祀により境内が手狭となったため、[[1952年]]（昭和27年）に境内の拡張と整備を行い、本殿などを新築{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。同年、白山神社を合祀。
[[1956年]]（昭和31年）、岐阜県神社庁よりに[[金幣社|銀幣社]]に指定される{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。

{{神社 |名称 = 丹生川神社 |画像 = |所在地 = [[岐阜県]][[高山市]]丹生川町坊方2373 |位置 = |祭神 = [[カグツチ|火結大神]] |社格 = [[金幣社|銀幣社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''丹生川神社'''（にゅうかわじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]丹生川町（旧・[[大野郡]][[丹生川村]]）に鎮座する[[神社]]。
神社名は[[大野郡]][[丹生川村]]に因む名であり、[[奈良県]]の[[丹生川上神社]]、[[丹生川上神社上社]]、[[丹生川上神社下社]]とは直接の関係は無い。

{{神社 |名称 = 丹生川神社 |画像 = |所在地 = [[岐阜県]][[高山市]]丹生川町坊方2373 |位置 = |祭神 = [[カグツチ|火結大神]] |社格 = [[金幣社|銀幣社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''丹生川神社'''（にゅうかわじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]丹生川町（旧・[[大野郡]][[丹生川村]]）に鎮座する[[神社]]。
神社名は[[大野郡]][[丹生川村]]に因む名であり、[[奈良県]]の[[丹生川上神社]]、[[丹生川上神社上社]]、[[丹生川上神社下社]]とは直接の関係は無い。
== 概要 == 元は丹生川村大字坊方に鎮座する八竈神社であったが、明治末期の[[神社合祀]]により村内の幾つかの神社が合祀され'''丹生川神社'''に改称した。
創建時期は不詳。かつては「八相の森」「八面荒神宮」と称していた{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}}。
[[1868年]]（明治元年）に八竈神社に改称{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}}。[[1871年]]（明治4年）に村社となる。
[[1907年]]（明治40年）、熊野神社及び貴船神社を合祀。同時に八竈神社から丹生川神社への改称を岐阜県に願い出、翌年許可を得る{{Sfn|丹生川村史|2000|p=436}}。[[1913年]]（大正2年）に熊野神社を合祀。[[1914年]]（大正3年）に'''丹生川神社'''に改称する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。
その後、昭和初期にかけて丹生川村内の神社（神明神社、熊野神社、諏訪神社、美女島神社など）を合祀する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。相次ぐ合祀により境内が手狭となったため、[[1952年]]（昭和27年）に境内の拡張と整備を行い、本殿などを新築{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。同年、白山神社を合祀。
[[1956年]]（昭和31年）、岐阜県神社庁よりに[[金幣社|銀幣社]]に指定される{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。
== 主祭神 == * [[カグツチ|火結大神]]
== 合祀{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}} == * [[かまど神|奥津日子大神]] * [[かまど神|奥津日女大神]] * 櫛御気大神 * 速玉男神 * 闇霧神 * [[天照大神|天照皇大神]] * [[大国主|大穴牟遅神]] * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]] * [[スサノオ|建速須佐男神]] * [[タケミナカタ|建御名方神]] * [[八坂刀売神]] * [[イチキシマヒメ|市杵嶋姫神]] * [[イザナギ|伊邪那岐神]] * [[イザナミ|伊邪那美神]] * [[菊理媛神]]
== 境外末社 == * [[招魂社]] : 旧・熊野神社（町方、1913年合祀）跡地{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}
== [[御旅所]] == * 町方（町方の旧・熊野神社（1913年合祀）跡地）と坊方（坊方の旧・熊野神社（1907年合祀）跡地）の2個所がある{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。
== 天然記念物 == * 丹生川神社の大杉（高山市指定天然記念物）<ref>{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=https://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000994/1004127.html |title=天然記念物一覧 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2025-05-02}}</ref>

revision.comment: /* 外部リンク */
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 丹生川神社 |画像 = |所在地 = [[岐阜県]][[高山市]]丹生川町坊方2373 |位置 = |祭神 = [[カグツチ|火結大神]] |社格 = [[金幣社|銀幣社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''丹生川神社'''（にゅうかわじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]丹生川町（旧・[[大野郡]][[丹生川村]]）に鎮座する[[神社]]。
神社名は[[大野郡]][[丹生川村]]に因む名であり、[[奈良県]]の[[丹生川上神社]]、[[丹生川上神社上社]]、[[丹生川上神社下社]]とは直接の関係は無い。
== 概要 == 元は丹生川村大字坊方に鎮座する八竈神社であったが、明治末期の[[神社合祀]]により村内の幾つかの神社が合祀され'''丹生川神社'''に改称した。
創建時期は不詳。かつては「八相の森」「八面荒神宮」と称していた{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}}。
[[1868年]]（明治元年）に八竈神社に改称{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}}。[[1871年]]（明治4年）に村社となる。
[[1907年]]（明治40年）、熊野神社及び貴船神社を合祀。同時に八竈神社から丹生川神社への改称を岐阜県に願い出、翌年許可を得る{{Sfn|丹生川村史|2000|p=436}}。[[1913年]]（大正2年）に熊野神社を合祀。[[1914年]]（大正3年）に'''丹生川神社'''に改称する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。
その後、昭和初期にかけて丹生川村内の神社（神明神社、熊野神社、諏訪神社、美女島神社など）を合祀する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。相次ぐ合祀により境内が手狭となったため、[[1952年]]（昭和27年）に境内の拡張と整備を行い、本殿などを新築{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。同年、白山神社を合祀。
[[1956年]]（昭和31年）、岐阜県神社庁よりに[[金幣社|銀幣社]]に指定される{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。
== 主祭神 == * [[カグツチ|火結大神]]
== 合祀{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}} == * [[かまど神|奥津日子大神]] * [[かまど神|奥津日女大神]] * 櫛御気大神 * 速玉男神 * 闇霧神 * [[天照大神|天照皇大神]] * [[大国主|大穴牟遅神]] * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]] * [[スサノオ|建速須佐男神]] * [[タケミナカタ|建御名方神]] * [[八坂刀売神]] * [[イチキシマヒメ|市杵嶋姫神]] * [[イザナギ|伊邪那岐神]] * [[イザナミ|伊邪那美神]] * [[菊理媛神]]
== 境外末社 == * [[招魂社]] : 旧・熊野神社（町方、1913年合祀）跡地{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}
== [[御旅所]] == * 町方（町方の旧・熊野神社（1913年合祀）跡地）と坊方（坊方の旧・熊野神社（1907年合祀）跡地）の2個所がある{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。
== 天然記念物 == * 丹生川神社の大杉（高山市指定天然記念物）<ref>{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=https://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000994/1004127.html |title=天然記念物一覧 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2025-05-02}}</ref>
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=土田吉左衛門 |title=飛騨の神社 |publisher=飛騨神職会 |date=1987-04 |id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000001983469}} |ref={{SfnRef|土田|1987}}}} * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=丹生川村史編集委員会 |title=丹生川村史 通史編 二 |publisher=丹生川村 |date=2000 |ref={{SfnRef|丹生川村史|2000}}}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2145 丹生川神社] - 岐阜県神社庁
{{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:にゆうかわしんしや}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧村社]] [[Category:高山市の歴史]] [[Category:高山市の建築物]]

== [[御旅所]] == * 町方（町方の旧・熊野神社（1913年合祀）跡地）と坊方（坊方の旧・熊野神社（1907年合祀）跡地）の2個所がある{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。
== 天然記念物 == * 丹生川神社の大杉（高山市指定天然記念物）<ref>{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=https://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000994/1004127.html |title=天然記念物一覧 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2025-05-02}}</ref>
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=土田吉左衛門 |title=飛騨の神社 |publisher=飛騨神職会 |date=1987-04 |id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000001983469}} |ref={{SfnRef|土田|1987}}}} * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=丹生川村史編集委員会 |title=丹生川村史 通史編 二 |publisher=丹生川村 |date=2000 |ref={{SfnRef|丹生川村史|2000}}}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2145 丹生川神社] - 岐阜県神社庁
{{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:にゆうかわしんしや}} [[Category:高山市の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧村社]]

revision.comment: 画像募集
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 丹生川神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=高山市}} |所在地 = [[岐阜県]][[高山市]]丹生川町坊方2373 |位置 = {{Coord|36|9|59.3|N|137|19|2.2|E|scale:10000_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[カグツチ|火結大神]] |社格 = [[金幣社|銀幣社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''丹生川神社'''（にゅうかわじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]丹生川町（旧・[[大野郡]][[丹生川村 (岐阜県)|丹生川村]]）に鎮座する[[神社]]。
神社名は[[大野郡]][[丹生川村 (岐阜県)|丹生川村]]に因む名であり、[[奈良県]]の[[丹生川上神社]]、[[丹生川上神社上社]]、[[丹生川上神社下社]]とは直接の関係は無い。
== 概要 == 元は丹生川村大字坊方に鎮座する八竈神社であったが、明治末期の[[神社合祀]]により村内の幾つかの神社が合祀され'''丹生川神社'''に改称した。
創建時期は不詳。かつては「八相の森」「八面荒神宮」と称していた{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}}。
[[1868年]]（明治元年）に八竈神社に改称{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}}。[[1871年]]（明治4年）に村社となる。
[[1907年]]（明治40年）、熊野神社及び貴船神社を合祀。同時に八竈神社から丹生川神社への改称を岐阜県に願い出、翌年許可を得る{{Sfn|丹生川村史|2000|p=436}}。[[1913年]]（大正2年）に熊野神社を合祀。[[1914年]]（大正3年）に'''丹生川神社'''に改称する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。
その後、昭和初期にかけて丹生川村内の神社（神明神社、熊野神社、諏訪神社、美女島神社など）を合祀する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。相次ぐ合祀により境内が手狭となったため、[[1952年]]（昭和27年）に境内の拡張と整備を行い、本殿などを新築{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。同年、白山神社を合祀。
[[1956年]]（昭和31年）、岐阜県神社庁よりに[[金幣社|銀幣社]]に指定される{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。
== 主祭神 == * [[カグツチ|火結大神]]
== 合祀{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}} == * [[かまど神|奥津日子大神]] * [[かまど神|奥津日女大神]] * 櫛御気大神 * 速玉男神 * 闇霧神 * [[天照大神|天照皇大神]] * [[大国主|大穴牟遅神]] * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]] * [[スサノオ|建速須佐男神]] * [[タケミナカタ|建御名方神]] * [[八坂刀売神]] * [[イチキシマヒメ|市杵嶋姫神]] * [[イザナギ|伊邪那岐神]] * [[イザナミ|伊邪那美神]] * [[菊理媛神]]
== 境外末社 == * [[招魂社]] : 旧・熊野神社（町方、1913年合祀）跡地{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}
== [[御旅所]] == * 町方（町方の旧・熊野神社（1913年合祀）跡地）と坊方（坊方の旧・熊野神社（1907年合祀）跡地）の2個所がある{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。
== 天然記念物 == * 丹生川神社の大杉（高山市指定天然記念物）<ref>{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=https://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000994/1004127.html |title=天然記念物一覧 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2025-05-02}}</ref>
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=土田吉左衛門 |title=飛騨の神社 |publisher=飛騨神職会 |date=1987-04 |id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000001983469}} |ref={{SfnRef|土田|1987}}}} * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=丹生川村史編集委員会 |title=丹生川村史 通史編 二 |publisher=丹生川村 |date=2000 |ref={{SfnRef|丹生川村史|2000}}}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2145 丹生川神社] - 岐阜県神社庁

{{nihongo|'''Nyuukawa Shrine (Uchi district)'''|丹生川神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nyuukawa Shrine (Uchi district)'''|丹生川神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{Infobox religious building

{{nihongo|'''Nyuukawa Shrine (Uchi district)'''|丹生川神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nyuukawa Shrine (Uchi district)'''|丹生川神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nyuukawa Shrine (Uchi district)'''|丹生川神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{Expand Japanese|丹生川神社}}

{{Infobox religious building
| name = Nyuukawa Shrine (Uchi district)

| mapframe = yes
| mapframe-zoom = 15

{{nihongo|'''Nyuukawa Shrine (Uchi district)'''|丹生川神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

revision.comment: 10 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 丹生川神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=高山市}} |所在地 = [[岐阜県]][[高山市]]丹生川町坊方2373 |位置 = {{Coord|36|9|59.3|N|137|19|2.2|E|scale:10000_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[カグツチ|火結大神]] |社格 = [[金幣社|銀幣社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''丹生川神社'''（にゅうかわじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]丹生川町（旧・[[大野郡]][[丹生川村 (岐阜県)|丹生川村]]）に鎮座する[[神社]]。
神社名は[[大野郡]][[丹生川村 (岐阜県)|丹生川村]]に因む名であり、[[奈良県]]の[[丹生川上神社]]、[[丹生川上神社上社]]、[[丹生川上神社下社]]とは直接の関係は無い。
== 概要 == 元は丹生川村大字坊方に鎮座する八竈神社であったが、明治末期の[[神社合祀]]により村内の幾つかの神社が合祀され'''丹生川神社'''に改称した。
創建時期は不詳。かつては「八相の森」「八面荒神宮」と称していた{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}}。
[[1868年]]（明治元年）に八竈神社に改称{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}}。[[1871年]]（明治4年）に村社となる。
[[1907年]]（明治40年）、熊野神社及び貴船神社を合祀。同時に八竈神社から丹生川神社への改称を岐阜県に願い出、翌年許可を得る{{Sfn|丹生川村史|2000|p=436}}。[[1913年]]（大正2年）に熊野神社を合祀。[[1914年]]（大正3年）に'''丹生川神社'''に改称する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。
その後、昭和初期にかけて丹生川村内の神社（神明神社、熊野神社、諏訪神社、美女島神社など）を合祀する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。相次ぐ合祀により境内が手狭となったため、[[1952年]]（昭和27年）に境内の拡張と整備を行い、本殿などを新築{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。同年、白山神社を合祀。
[[1956年]]（昭和31年）、岐阜県神社庁よりに[[金幣社|銀幣社]]に指定される{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。
== 主祭神 == * [[カグツチ|火結大神]]
== 合祀{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}} == * [[かまど神|奥津日子大神]] * [[かまど神|奥津日女大神]] * 櫛御気大神 * 速玉男神 * 闇霧神 * [[天照大神|天照皇大神]] * [[大国主|大穴牟遅神]] * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]] * [[スサノオ|建速須佐男神]] * [[タケミナカタ|建御名方神]] * [[八坂刀売神]] * [[イチキシマヒメ|市杵嶋姫神]] * [[イザナギ|伊邪那岐神]] * [[イザナミ|伊邪那美神]] * [[菊理媛神]]
== 境外末社 == * [[招魂社]] : 旧・熊野神社（町方、1913年合祀）跡地{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}
== [[御旅所]] == * 町方（町方の旧・熊野神社（1913年合祀）跡地）と坊方（坊方の旧・熊野神社（1907年合祀）跡地）の2個所がある{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。
== 天然記念物 == * 丹生川神社の大杉（高山市指定天然記念物）<ref>{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=https://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000994/1004127.html |title=天然記念物一覧 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2025-05-02}}</ref>
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=土田吉左衛門 |title=飛騨の神社 |publisher=飛騨神職会 |date=1987-04 |id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000001983469}} |ref={{SfnRef|土田|1987}}}} * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=丹生川村史編集委員会 |title=丹生川村史 通史編 二 |publisher=丹生川村 |date=2000 |ref={{SfnRef|丹生川村史|2000}}}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2145 丹生川神社] - 岐阜県神社庁
{{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:にゆうかわしんしや}} [[Category:高山市の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧村社]]

{{translated page|ja|丹生川神社|version=104888527|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 丹生川神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=高山市}} |所在地 = [[岐阜県]][[高山市]]丹生川町坊方2373 |位置 = {{Coord|36|9|59.3|N|137|19|2.2|E|scale:10000_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[カグツチ|火結大神]] |社格 = [[金幣社|銀幣社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''丹生川神社'''（にゅうかわじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]丹生川町（旧・[[大野郡]][[丹生川村 (岐阜県)|丹生川村]]）に鎮座する[[神社]]。
神社名は[[大野郡]][[丹生川村 (岐阜県)|丹生川村]]に因む名であり、[[奈良県]]の[[丹生川上神社]]、[[丹生川上神社上社]]、[[丹生川上神社下社]]とは直接の関係は無い。
== 概要 == 元は丹生川村大字坊方に鎮座する八竈神社であったが、明治末期の[[神社合祀]]により村内の幾つかの神社が合祀され'''丹生川神社'''に改称した。
創建時期は不詳。かつては「八相の森」「八面荒神宮」と称していた{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}}。
[[1868年]]（明治元年）に八竈神社に改称{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}}。[[1871年]]（明治4年）に村社となる。
[[1907年]]（明治40年）、熊野神社及び貴船神社を合祀。同時に八竈神社から丹生川神社への改称を岐阜県に願い出、翌年許可を得る{{Sfn|丹生川村史|2000|p=436}}。[[1913年]]（大正2年）に熊野神社を合祀。[[1914年]]（大正3年）に'''丹生川神社'''に改称する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。
その後、昭和初期にかけて丹生川村内の神社（神明神社、熊野神社、諏訪神社、美女島神社など）を合祀する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。相次ぐ合祀により境内が手狭となったため、[[1952年]]（昭和27年）に境内の拡張と整備を行い、本殿などを新築{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。同年、白山神社を合祀。
[[1956年]]（昭和31年）、岐阜県神社庁よりに[[金幣社|銀幣社]]に指定される{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。
== 主祭神 == * [[カグツチ|火結大神]]
== 合祀{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}} == * [[かまど神|奥津日子大神]] * [[かまど神|奥津日女大神]] * 櫛御気大神 * 速玉男神 * 闇霧神 * [[天照大神|天照皇大神]] * [[大国主|大穴牟遅神]] * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]] * [[スサノオ|建速須佐男神]] * [[タケミナカタ|建御名方神]] * [[八坂刀売神]] * [[イチキシマヒメ|市杵嶋姫神]] * [[イザナギ|伊邪那岐神]] * [[イザナミ|伊邪那美神]] * [[菊理媛神]]
== 境外末社 == * [[招魂社]] : 旧・熊野神社（町方、1913年合祀）跡地{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}
== [[御旅所]] == * 町方（町方の旧・熊野神社（1913年合祀）跡地）と坊方（坊方の旧・熊野神社（1907年合祀）跡地）の2個所がある{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。
== 天然記念物 == * 丹生川神社の大杉（高山市指定天然記念物）<ref>{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=https://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000994/1004127.html |title=天然記念物一覧 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2025-05-02}}</ref>
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=土田吉左衛門 |title=飛騨の神社 |publisher=飛騨神職会 |date=1987-04 |id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000001983469}} |ref={{SfnRef|土田|1987}}}} * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=丹生川村史編集委員会 |title=丹生川村史 通史編 二 |publisher=丹生川村 |date=2000 |ref={{SfnRef|丹生川村史|2000}}}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2145 丹生川神社] - 岐阜県神社庁
{{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:にゆうかわしんしや}} [[Category:高山市の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧村社]]
==End Japanese==

{{translated page|ja|丹生川神社|version=104888527|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 丹生川神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=高山市}} |所在地 = [[岐阜県]][[高山市]]丹生川町坊方2373 |位置 = {{Coord|36|9|59.3|N|137|19|2.2|E|scale:10000_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[カグツチ|火結大神]] |社格 = [[金幣社|銀幣社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''丹生川神社'''（にゅうかわじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]丹生川町（旧・[[大野郡]][[丹生川村 (岐阜県)|丹生川村]]）に鎮座する[[神社]]。
神社名は[[大野郡]][[丹生川村 (岐阜県)|丹生川村]]に因む名であり、[[奈良県]]の[[丹生川上神社]]、[[丹生川上神社上社]]、[[丹生川上神社下社]]とは直接の関係は無い。
== 概要 == 元は丹生川村大字坊方に鎮座する八竈神社であったが、明治末期の[[神社合祀]]により村内の幾つかの神社が合祀され'''丹生川神社'''に改称した。
創建時期は不詳。かつては「八相の森」「八面荒神宮」と称していた{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}}。
[[1868年]]（明治元年）に八竈神社に改称{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}}。[[1871年]]（明治4年）に村社となる。
[[1907年]]（明治40年）、熊野神社及び貴船神社を合祀。同時に八竈神社から丹生川神社への改称を岐阜県に願い出、翌年許可を得る{{Sfn|丹生川村史|2000|p=436}}。[[1913年]]（大正2年）に熊野神社を合祀。[[1914年]]（大正3年）に'''丹生川神社'''に改称する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。
その後、昭和初期にかけて丹生川村内の神社（神明神社、熊野神社、諏訪神社、美女島神社など）を合祀する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。相次ぐ合祀により境内が手狭となったため、[[1952年]]（昭和27年）に境内の拡張と整備を行い、本殿などを新築{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。同年、白山神社を合祀。
[[1956年]]（昭和31年）、岐阜県神社庁よりに[[金幣社|銀幣社]]に指定される{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。
== 主祭神 == * [[カグツチ|火結大神]]
== 合祀{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}} == * [[かまど神|奥津日子大神]] * [[かまど神|奥津日女大神]] * 櫛御気大神 * 速玉男神 * 闇霧神 * [[天照大神|天照皇大神]] * [[大国主|大穴牟遅神]] * [[スクナビコナ|少彦名神]] * [[スサノオ|建速須佐男神]] * [[タケミナカタ|建御名方神]] * [[八坂刀売神]] * [[イチキシマヒメ|市杵嶋姫神]] * [[イザナギ|伊邪那岐神]] * [[イザナミ|伊邪那美神]] * [[菊理媛神]]
== 境外末社 == * [[招魂社]] : 旧・熊野神社（町方、1913年合祀）跡地{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}
== [[御旅所]] == * 町方（町方の旧・熊野神社（1913年合祀）跡地）と坊方（坊方の旧・熊野神社（1907年合祀）跡地）の2個所がある{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。
== 天然記念物 == * 丹生川神社の大杉（高山市指定天然記念物）<ref>{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=https://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000994/1004127.html |title=天然記念物一覧 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2025-05-02}}</ref>
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=土田吉左衛門 |title=飛騨の神社 |publisher=飛騨神職会 |date=1987-04 |id={{国立国会図書館書誌ID|000001983469}} |ref={{SfnRef|土田|1987}}}} * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=丹生川村史編集委員会 |title=丹生川村史 通史編 二 |publisher=丹生川村 |date=2000 |ref={{SfnRef|丹生川村史|2000}}}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2145 丹生川神社] - 岐阜県神社庁
{{shinto-stub}}
==End Japanese== [[Category:高山市の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧村社]]

'''丹生川神社'''（にゅうかわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|高山市|Q622594}}丹生川町（旧・{{jalink|大野郡|Q31671}}{{jalink|丹生川村 (岐阜県)|Q7071339|丹生川村}}）に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

神社名は{{jalink|大野郡|Q31671}}{{jalink|丹生川村 (岐阜県)|Q7071339|丹生川村}}に因む名であり、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}の{{jalink|丹生川上神社|Q246370}}、{{jalink|丹生川上神社上社|Q10877366}}、{{jalink|丹生川上神社下社|Q10877367}}とは直接の関係は無い。

== 概要 ==
元は丹生川村大字坊方に鎮座する八竈神社であったが、明治末期の{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700}}により村内の幾つかの神社が合祀され'''丹生川神社'''に改称した。

創建時期は不詳。かつては「八相の森」「八面荒神宮」と称していた{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}}。

{{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}（明治元年）に八竈神社に改称{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}}。{{jalink|1871年|Q7746}}（明治4年）に村社となる。

{{jalink|1907年|Q2048}}（明治40年）、熊野神社及び貴船神社を合祀。同時に八竈神社から丹生川神社への改称を岐阜県に願い出、翌年許可を得る{{Sfn|丹生川村史|2000|p=436}}。{{jalink|1913年|Q2080}}（大正2年）に熊野神社を合祀。{{jalink|1914年|Q2083}}（大正3年）に'''丹生川神社'''に改称する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。

その後、昭和初期にかけて丹生川村内の神社（神明神社、熊野神社、諏訪神社、美女島神社など）を合祀する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。相次ぐ合祀により境内が手狭となったため、{{jalink|1952年|Q5272}}（昭和27年）に境内の拡張と整備を行い、本殿などを新築{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。同年、白山神社を合祀。

{{jalink|1956年|Q5221}}（昭和31年）、岐阜県神社庁よりに{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592|銀幣社}}に指定される{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。

== 主祭神 ==
* {{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|火結大神}}

== 合祀{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}} ==
* {{jalink|かまど神|Q249178|奥津日子大神}}
* {{jalink|かまど神|Q249178|奥津日女大神}}

* 速玉男神
* 闇霧神
* {{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|天照皇大神}}
* {{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大穴牟遅神}}
* {{jalink|スクナビコナ|Q3275687|少彦名神}}
* {{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|建速須佐男神}}
* {{jalink|タケミナカタ|Q9150391|建御名方神}}
* {{jalink|八坂刀売神|Q9150392}}
* {{jalink|イチキシマヒメ|Q11059454|市杵嶋姫神}}
* {{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊邪那岐神}}
* {{jalink|イザナミ|Q682306|伊邪那美神}}
* {{jalink|菊理媛神|Q11619891}}

== 境外末社 ==
* {{jalink|招魂社|Q11497810}}
: 旧・熊野神社（町方、1913年合祀）跡地{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}

== {{jalink|御旅所|Q11489117}} ==
* 町方（町方の旧・熊野神社（1913年合祀）跡地）と坊方（坊方の旧・熊野神社（1907年合祀）跡地）の2個所がある{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。

== 天然記念物 ==
* 丹生川神社の大杉（高山市指定天然記念物）<ref>{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=https://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000994/1004127.html |title=天然記念物一覧 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2025-05-02}}</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Nyuukawa Shrine (Uchi district)'''|丹生川神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 丹生川神社
|画像   = {{画像募集中|cat=高山市}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|高山市|Q622594}}丹生川町坊方2373
|位置   = {{Coord|36|9|59.3|N|137|19|2.2|E|scale:10000_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = {{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|火結大神}}
|社格   = {{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592|銀幣社}}
|創建   = 不詳

|神事   =

'''丹生川神社'''（にゅうかわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|高山市|Q622594}}丹生川町（旧・{{jalink|大野郡|Q31671}}{{jalink|丹生川村 (岐阜県)|Q7071339|丹生川村}}）に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

神社名は{{jalink|大野郡|Q31671}}{{jalink|丹生川村 (岐阜県)|Q7071339|丹生川村}}に因む名であり、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}の{{jalink|丹生川上神社|Q246370}}、{{jalink|丹生川上神社上社|Q10877366}}、{{jalink|丹生川上神社下社|Q10877367}}とは直接の関係は無い。

=== 概要 ===
元は丹生川村大字坊方に鎮座する八竈神社であったが、明治末期の{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700}}により村内の幾つかの神社が合祀され'''丹生川神社'''に改称した。

創建時期は不詳。かつては「八相の森」「八面荒神宮」と称していた{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}}。

{{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}（明治元年）に八竈神社に改称{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}}。{{jalink|1871年|Q7746}}（明治4年）に村社となる。

{{jalink|1907年|Q2048}}（明治40年）、熊野神社及び貴船神社を合祀。同時に八竈神社から丹生川神社への改称を岐阜県に願い出、翌年許可を得る{{Sfn|丹生川村史|2000|p=436}}。{{jalink|1913年|Q2080}}（大正2年）に熊野神社を合祀。{{jalink|1914年|Q2083}}（大正3年）に'''丹生川神社'''に改称する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。

その後、昭和初期にかけて丹生川村内の神社（神明神社、熊野神社、諏訪神社、美女島神社など）を合祀する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。相次ぐ合祀により境内が手狭となったため、{{jalink|1952年|Q5272}}（昭和27年）に境内の拡張と整備を行い、本殿などを新築{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。同年、白山神社を合祀。

{{jalink|1956年|Q5221}}（昭和31年）、岐阜県神社庁よりに{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592|銀幣社}}に指定される{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。

* 町方（町方の旧・熊野神社（1913年合祀）跡地）と坊方（坊方の旧・熊野神社（1907年合祀）跡地）の2個所がある{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。

{{nihongo|'''Nyuukawa Shrine (Uchi district)'''|丹生川神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 丹生川神社
|画像   = {{画像募集中|cat=高山市}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|高山市|Q622594}}丹生川町坊方2373
|位置   = {{Coord|36|9|59.3|N|137|19|2.2|E|scale:10000_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = {{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|火結大神}}
|社格   = {{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592|銀幣社}}
|創建   = 不詳
|本殿   = 
|別名   = 
|札所等 = 
|例祭   = 
|神事   = 
}}
'''丹生川神社'''（にゅうかわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|高山市|Q622594}}丹生川町（旧・{{jalink|大野郡|Q31671}}{{jalink|丹生川村 (岐阜県)|Q7071339|丹生川村}}）に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

神社名は{{jalink|大野郡|Q31671}}{{jalink|丹生川村 (岐阜県)|Q7071339|丹生川村}}に因む名であり、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}の{{jalink|丹生川上神社|Q246370}}、{{jalink|丹生川上神社上社|Q10877366}}、{{jalink|丹生川上神社下社|Q10877367}}とは直接の関係は無い。

=== 概要 ===
元は丹生川村大字坊方に鎮座する八竈神社であったが、明治末期の{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700}}により村内の幾つかの神社が合祀され'''丹生川神社'''に改称した。

創建時期は不詳。かつては「八相の森」「八面荒神宮」と称していた{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}}。

{{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}（明治元年）に八竈神社に改称{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}}。{{jalink|1871年|Q7746}}（明治4年）に村社となる。

{{jalink|1907年|Q2048}}（明治40年）、熊野神社及び貴船神社を合祀。同時に八竈神社から丹生川神社への改称を岐阜県に願い出、翌年許可を得る{{Sfn|丹生川村史|2000|p=436}}。{{jalink|1913年|Q2080}}（大正2年）に熊野神社を合祀。{{jalink|1914年|Q2083}}（大正3年）に'''丹生川神社'''に改称する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。

その後、昭和初期にかけて丹生川村内の神社（神明神社、熊野神社、諏訪神社、美女島神社など）を合祀する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。相次ぐ合祀により境内が手狭となったため、{{jalink|1952年|Q5272}}（昭和27年）に境内の拡張と整備を行い、本殿などを新築{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。同年、白山神社を合祀。

{{jalink|1956年|Q5221}}（昭和31年）、岐阜県神社庁よりに{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592|銀幣社}}に指定される{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。

{{nihongo|'''Nyuukawa Shrine (Uchi district)'''|丹生川神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 丹生川神社
|画像   = {{画像募集中|cat=高山市}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|高山市|Q622594}}丹生川町坊方2373
|位置   = {{Coord|36|9|59.3|N|137|19|2.2|E|scale:10000_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = {{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|火結大神}}
|社格   = {{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592|銀幣社}}
|創建   = 不詳
|本殿   = 
|別名   = 
|札所等 = 
|例祭   = 
|神事   = 
}}
'''丹生川神社'''（にゅうかわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|高山市|Q622594}}丹生川町（旧・{{jalink|大野郡|Q31671}}{{jalink|丹生川村 (岐阜県)|Q7071339|丹生川村}}）に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

神社名は{{jalink|大野郡|Q31671}}{{jalink|丹生川村 (岐阜県)|Q7071339|丹生川村}}に因む名であり、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}の{{jalink|丹生川上神社|Q246370}}、{{jalink|丹生川上神社上社|Q10877366}}、{{jalink|丹生川上神社下社|Q10877367}}とは直接の関係は無い。

=== 概要 ===
元は丹生川村大字坊方に鎮座する八竈神社であったが、明治末期の{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700}}により村内の幾つかの神社が合祀され'''丹生川神社'''に改称した。

創建時期は不詳。かつては「八相の森」「八面荒神宮」と称していた{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}}。

{{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}（明治元年）に八竈神社に改称{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}}。{{jalink|1871年|Q7746}}（明治4年）に村社となる。

{{jalink|1907年|Q2048}}（明治40年）、熊野神社及び貴船神社を合祀。同時に八竈神社から丹生川神社への改称を岐阜県に願い出、翌年許可を得る{{Sfn|丹生川村史|2000|p=436}}。{{jalink|1913年|Q2080}}（大正2年）に熊野神社を合祀。{{jalink|1914年|Q2083}}（大正3年）に'''丹生川神社'''に改称する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。

その後、昭和初期にかけて丹生川村内の神社（神明神社、熊野神社、諏訪神社、美女島神社など）を合祀する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。相次ぐ合祀により境内が手狭となったため、{{jalink|1952年|Q5272}}（昭和27年）に境内の拡張と整備を行い、本殿などを新築{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。同年、白山神社を合祀。

{{jalink|1956年|Q5221}}（昭和31年）、岐阜県神社庁よりに{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592|銀幣社}}に指定される{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。

{{nihongo|'''Nyuukawa Shrine (Uchi district)'''|丹生川神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

[[JA:丹生川神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q134927823}}

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 丹生川神社
|画像   = {{画像募集中|cat=高山市}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|高山市|Q622594}}丹生川町坊方2373
|位置   = {{Coord|36|9|59.3|N|137|19|2.2|E|scale:10000_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = {{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|火結大神}}
|社格   = {{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592|銀幣社}}
|創建   = 不詳
|本殿   = 
|別名   = 
|札所等 = 
|例祭   = 
|神事   = 
}}
'''丹生川神社'''（にゅうかわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|高山市|Q622594}}丹生川町（旧・{{jalink|大野郡|Q31671}}{{jalink|丹生川村 (岐阜県)|Q7071339|丹生川村}}）に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

神社名は{{jalink|大野郡|Q31671}}{{jalink|丹生川村 (岐阜県)|Q7071339|丹生川村}}に因む名であり、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}の{{jalink|丹生川上神社|Q246370}}、{{jalink|丹生川上神社上社|Q10877366}}、{{jalink|丹生川上神社下社|Q10877367}}とは直接の関係は無い。

=== 概要 ===
元は丹生川村大字坊方に鎮座する八竈神社であったが、明治末期の{{jalink|神社合祀|Q11590700}}により村内の幾つかの神社が合祀され'''丹生川神社'''に改称した。

創建時期は不詳。かつては「八相の森」「八面荒神宮」と称していた{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}}。

{{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}（明治元年）に八竈神社に改称{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=43}}。{{jalink|1871年|Q7746}}（明治4年）に村社となる。

{{jalink|1907年|Q2048}}（明治40年）、熊野神社及び貴船神社を合祀。同時に八竈神社から丹生川神社への改称を岐阜県に願い出、翌年許可を得る{{Sfn|丹生川村史|2000|p=436}}。{{jalink|1913年|Q2080}}（大正2年）に熊野神社を合祀。{{jalink|1914年|Q2083}}（大正3年）に'''丹生川神社'''に改称する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。

その後、昭和初期にかけて丹生川村内の神社（神明神社、熊野神社、諏訪神社、美女島神社など）を合祀する{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。相次ぐ合祀により境内が手狭となったため、{{jalink|1952年|Q5272}}（昭和27年）に境内の拡張と整備を行い、本殿などを新築{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。同年、白山神社を合祀。

{{jalink|1956年|Q5221}}（昭和31年）、岐阜県神社庁よりに{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592|銀幣社}}に指定される{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。

=== 主祭神 ===

=== 境外末社 ===

=== {{jalink|御旅所|Q11489117}} ===
* 町方（町方の旧・熊野神社（1913年合祀）跡地）と坊方（坊方の旧・熊野神社（1907年合祀）跡地）の2個所がある{{Sfn|土田|1987|p=44}}。

{{nihongo|'''Nyuukawa Shrine (Uchi district)'''|丹生川神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{Shinto shrine
|Name        = Nyūkawa Shrine
|Image       = {{Image wanted|cat=Takayama City}}
|Location    = {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Takayama City|Q622594}} 2373 Bokata, Nyūkawa-chō
|Coordinates = {{Coord|36|9|59.3|N|137|19|2.2|E|scale:10000_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}}
|Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Himusubi Ōkami}}
|Shrine rank = {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592|Ginpeisha}}
|Established = Unknown
|Main hall   = 
|Alternate name =

}}
**Nyūkawa Shrine** (にゅうかわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Nyūkawa-chō (formerly {{jalink|Ōno District|Q31671}}{{jalink|Nyūkawa Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q7071339|Nyūkawa Village}}), {{jalink|Takayama City|Q622594}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}.

The shrine’s name is derived from {{jalink|Nyūkawa Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q7071339|Nyūkawa Village}} in {{jalink|Ōno District|Q31671}} and has no direct relation to {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}’s {{jalink|Niukawakami Shrine|Q246370}}, {{jalink|Niukawakami Shrine Upper Shrine|Q10877366}}, or {{jalink|Niukawakami Shrine Lower Shrine|Q10877367}}.

=== Overview ===
Originally, it was Yakama Shrine, enshrined in the Bokata district of Nyūkawa Village. During the shrine consolidation policy ({{jalink|Shinto shrine consolidation|Q11590700}}) in the late Meiji period, several shrines within the village were merged, and it was renamed **Nyūkawa Shrine**.

The date of establishment is unknown. In the past, it was known as “Yasō no Mori” and “Hachimen Aragū-gū” {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}}.

In {{jalink|1868|Q7717}} (Meiji 1), it was renamed Yakama Shrine {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}}. In {{jalink|1871|Q7746}} (Meiji 4), it became a village shrine.

In {{jalink|1907|Q2048}} (Meiji 40), Kumano Shrine and Kifune Shrine were merged into it. At the same time, an application was submitted to Gifu Prefecture to change the name from Yakama Shrine to Nyūkawa Shrine, which was approved the following year {{Sfn|Nyūkawa Village History|2000|p=436}}. In {{jalink|1913|Q2080}} (Taishō 2), Kumano Shrine was merged. In {{jalink|1914|Q2083}} (Taishō 3), it was officially renamed **Nyūkawa Shrine** {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

Thereafter, through the early Shōwa period, shrines within Nyūkawa Village (including Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Suwa Shrine, and Bijoshima Shrine) were successively merged {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}. As repeated mergers made the precincts cramped, the grounds were expanded and improved in {{jalink|1952|Q5272}} (Shōwa 27), and a new main hall and other buildings were constructed {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}. In the same year, Hakusan Shrine was merged.

In {{jalink|1956|Q5221}} (Shōwa 31), it was designated a {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592|Ginpeisha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

=== Main enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Himusubi Ōkami}}

* {{jalink|Kamado-gami|Q249178|Okutsuhime Ōkami}}

* Hayatamao no Kami
* Yamikiri no Kami

=== Subsidiary shrines outside the precincts ===
* {{jalink|Shōkonsha|Q11497810}}
: Located on the former site of Kumano Shrine (Machikata, merged in 1913) {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}

=== {{jalink|Otabisho|Q11489117}} ===
* There are two locations: Machikata (former site of Kumano Shrine in Machikata, merged in 1913) and Bokata (former site of Kumano Shrine in Bokata, merged in 1907) {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

=== Natural monument ===
* The Great Cedar of Nyūkawa Shrine (designated natural monument of Takayama City) <ref>{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=https://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000994/1004127.html |title=List of Natural Monuments |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2025-05-02}}</ref>

== Japanese content ==
{{Shinto shrine
|Name        = Nyūkawa Shrine
|Image       = {{Image wanted|cat=Takayama City}}
|Location    = {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Takayama City|Q622594}} 2373 Bokata, Nyūkawa-chō
|Coordinates = {{Coord|36|9|59.3|N|137|19|2.2|E|scale:10000_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}}
|Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Himusubi Ōkami}}
|Shrine rank = {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592|Ginpeisha}}
|Established = Unknown
|Main hall   = 
|Alternate name = 
|Pilgrimage sites = 
|Annual festival = 
|Rituals     = 
}}
**Nyūkawa Shrine** (にゅうかわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Nyūkawa-chō (formerly {{jalink|Ōno District|Q31671}}{{jalink|Nyūkawa Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q7071339|Nyūkawa Village}}), {{jalink|Takayama City|Q622594}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}.

The shrine’s name is derived from {{jalink|Nyūkawa Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q7071339|Nyūkawa Village}} in {{jalink|Ōno District|Q31671}} and has no direct relation to {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}’s {{jalink|Niukawakami Shrine|Q246370}}, {{jalink|Niukawakami Shrine Upper Shrine|Q10877366}}, or {{jalink|Niukawakami Shrine Lower Shrine|Q10877367}}.

=== Overview ===
Originally, it was Yakama Shrine, enshrined in the Bokata district of Nyūkawa Village. During the shrine consolidation policy ({{jalink|Shinto shrine consolidation|Q11590700}}) in the late Meiji period, several shrines within the village were merged, and it was renamed **Nyūkawa Shrine**.

The date of establishment is unknown. In the past, it was known as “Yasō no Mori” and “Hachimen Aragū-gū” {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}}.

In {{jalink|1868|Q7717}} (Meiji 1), it was renamed Yakama Shrine {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}}. In {{jalink|1871|Q7746}} (Meiji 4), it became a village shrine.

In {{jalink|1907|Q2048}} (Meiji 40), Kumano Shrine and Kifune Shrine were merged into it. At the same time, an application was submitted to Gifu Prefecture to change the name from Yakama Shrine to Nyūkawa Shrine, which was approved the following year {{Sfn|Nyūkawa Village History|2000|p=436}}. In {{jalink|1913|Q2080}} (Taishō 2), Kumano Shrine was merged. In {{jalink|1914|Q2083}} (Taishō 3), it was officially renamed **Nyūkawa Shrine** {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

Thereafter, through the early Shōwa period, shrines within Nyūkawa Village (including Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Suwa Shrine, and Bijoshima Shrine) were successively merged {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}. As repeated mergers made the precincts cramped, the grounds were expanded and improved in {{jalink|1952|Q5272}} (Shōwa 27), and a new main hall and other buildings were constructed {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}. In the same year, Hakusan Shrine was merged.

In {{jalink|1956|Q5221}} (Shōwa 31), it was designated a {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592|Ginpeisha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

=== Main enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Himusubi Ōkami}}

=== Merged deities {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}} ===
* {{jalink|Kamado-gami|Q249178|Okutsuhiko Ōkami}}
* {{jalink|Kamado-gami|Q249178|Okutsuhime Ōkami}}
* Kushimiké Ōkami
* Hayatamao no Kami
* Yamikiri no Kami
* {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu Ōmikami}}
* {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōanamuchi no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukuhikona no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Takehayasusanoo no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Takeminakata|Q9150391|Takeminakata no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Yasakatome no Kami|Q9150392}}
* {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Kikurihime|Q11619891}}

=== Subsidiary shrines outside the precincts ===
* {{jalink|Shōkonsha|Q11497810}}
: Located on the former site of Kumano Shrine (Machikata, merged in 1913) {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}

=== {{jalink|Otabisho|Q11489117}} ===
* There are two locations: Machikata (former site of Kumano Shrine in Machikata, merged in 1913) and Bokata (former site of Kumano Shrine in Bokata, merged in 1907) {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

=== Natural monument ===
* The Great Cedar of Nyūkawa Shrine (designated natural monument of Takayama City) <ref>{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=https://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000994/1004127.html |title=List of Natural Monuments |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2025-05-02}}</ref>

== Japanese content ==
{{Shinto shrine
|Name        = Nyūkawa Shrine
|Image       = {{Image wanted|cat=Takayama City}}
|Location    = {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Takayama City|Q622594}} 2373 Bokata, Nyūkawa-chō
|Coordinates = {{Coord|36|9|59.3|N|137|19|2.2|E|scale:10000_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}}
|Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Himusubi Ōkami}}
|Shrine rank = {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592|Ginpeisha}}
|Established = Unknown
|Main hall   = 
|Alternate name = 
|Pilgrimage sites = 
|Annual festival = 
|Rituals     = 
}}
**Nyūkawa Shrine** (にゅうかわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Nyūkawa-chō (formerly {{jalink|Ōno District|Q31671}}{{jalink|Nyūkawa Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q7071339|Nyūkawa Village}}), {{jalink|Takayama City|Q622594}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}.

The shrine’s name is derived from {{jalink|Nyūkawa Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q7071339|Nyūkawa Village}} in {{jalink|Ōno District|Q31671}} and has no direct relation to {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}’s {{jalink|Niukawakami Shrine|Q246370}}, {{jalink|Niukawakami Shrine Upper Shrine|Q10877366}}, or {{jalink|Niukawakami Shrine Lower Shrine|Q10877367}}.

=== Overview ===
Originally, it was Yakama Shrine, enshrined in the Bokata district of Nyūkawa Village. During the shrine consolidation policy ({{jalink|Shinto shrine consolidation|Q11590700}}) in the late Meiji period, several shrines within the village were merged, and it was renamed **Nyūkawa Shrine**.

The date of establishment is unknown. In the past, it was known as “Yasō no Mori” and “Hachimen Aragū-gū” {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}}.

In {{jalink|1868|Q7717}} (Meiji 1), it was renamed Yakama Shrine {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}}. In {{jalink|1871|Q7746}} (Meiji 4), it became a village shrine.

In {{jalink|1907|Q2048}} (Meiji 40), Kumano Shrine and Kifune Shrine were merged into it. At the same time, an application was submitted to Gifu Prefecture to change the name from Yakama Shrine to Nyūkawa Shrine, which was approved the following year {{Sfn|Nyūkawa Village History|2000|p=436}}. In {{jalink|1913|Q2080}} (Taishō 2), Kumano Shrine was merged. In {{jalink|1914|Q2083}} (Taishō 3), it was officially renamed **Nyūkawa Shrine** {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

Thereafter, through the early Shōwa period, shrines within Nyūkawa Village (including Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Suwa Shrine, and Bijoshima Shrine) were successively merged {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}. As repeated mergers made the precincts cramped, the grounds were expanded and improved in {{jalink|1952|Q5272}} (Shōwa 27), and a new main hall and other buildings were constructed {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}. In the same year, Hakusan Shrine was merged.

In {{jalink|1956|Q5221}} (Shōwa 31), it was designated a {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592|Ginpeisha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

=== Main enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Himusubi Ōkami}}

=== Merged deities {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}} ===
* {{jalink|Kamado-gami|Q249178|Okutsuhiko Ōkami}}
* {{jalink|Kamado-gami|Q249178|Okutsuhime Ōkami}}
* Kushimiké Ōkami
* Hayatamao no Kami
* Yamikiri no Kami
* {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu Ōmikami}}
* {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōanamuchi no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukuhikona no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Takehayasusanoo no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Takeminakata|Q9150391|Takeminakata no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Yasakatome no Kami|Q9150392}}
* {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Kikurihime|Q11619891}}

=== Subsidiary shrines outside the precincts ===
* {{jalink|Shōkonsha|Q11497810}}
: Located on the former site of Kumano Shrine (Machikata, merged in 1913) {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}

=== {{jalink|Otabisho|Q11489117}} ===
* There are two locations: Machikata (former site of Kumano Shrine in Machikata, merged in 1913) and Bokata (former site of Kumano Shrine in Bokata, merged in 1907) {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

=== Natural monument ===
* The Great Cedar of Nyūkawa Shrine (designated natural monument of Takayama City) <ref>{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=https://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000994/1004127.html |title=List of Natural Monuments |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2025-05-02}}</ref>

== Japanese content ==
{{Shinto shrine
|Name        = Nyūkawa Shrine
|Image       = {{Image wanted|cat=Takayama City}}
|Location    = {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Takayama City|Q622594}} 2373 Bokata, Nyūkawa-chō
|Coordinates = {{Coord|36|9|59.3|N|137|19|2.2|E|scale:10000_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}}
|Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Himusubi Ōkami}}
|Shrine rank = {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592|Ginpeisha}}
|Established = Unknown
|Main hall   = 
|Alternate name = 
|Pilgrimage sites = 
|Annual festival = 
|Rituals     = 
}}
**Nyūkawa Shrine** (にゅうかわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Nyūkawa-chō (formerly {{jalink|Ōno District|Q31671}}{{jalink|Nyūkawa Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q7071339|Nyūkawa Village}}), {{jalink|Takayama City|Q622594}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}.

The shrine’s name is derived from {{jalink|Nyūkawa Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q7071339|Nyūkawa Village}} in {{jalink|Ōno District|Q31671}} and has no direct relation to {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}’s {{jalink|Niukawakami Shrine|Q246370}}, {{jalink|Niukawakami Shrine Upper Shrine|Q10877366}}, or {{jalink|Niukawakami Shrine Lower Shrine|Q10877367}}.

=== Overview ===
Originally, it was Yakama Shrine, enshrined in the Bokata district of Nyūkawa Village. During the shrine consolidation policy ({{jalink|Shinto shrine consolidation|Q11590700}}) in the late Meiji period, several shrines within the village were merged, and it was renamed **Nyūkawa Shrine**.

The date of establishment is unknown. In the past, it was known as “Yasō no Mori” and “Hachimen Aragū-gū” {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}}.

In {{jalink|1868|Q7717}} (Meiji 1), it was renamed Yakama Shrine {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}}. In {{jalink|1871|Q7746}} (Meiji 4), it became a village shrine.

In {{jalink|1907|Q2048}} (Meiji 40), Kumano Shrine and Kifune Shrine were merged into it. At the same time, an application was submitted to Gifu Prefecture to change the name from Yakama Shrine to Nyūkawa Shrine, which was approved the following year {{Sfn|Nyūkawa Village History|2000|p=436}}. In {{jalink|1913|Q2080}} (Taishō 2), Kumano Shrine was merged. In {{jalink|1914|Q2083}} (Taishō 3), it was officially renamed **Nyūkawa Shrine** {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

Thereafter, through the early Shōwa period, shrines within Nyūkawa Village (including Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Suwa Shrine, and Bijoshima Shrine) were successively merged {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}. As repeated mergers made the precincts cramped, the grounds were expanded and improved in {{jalink|1952|Q5272}} (Shōwa 27), and a new main hall and other buildings were constructed {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}. In the same year, Hakusan Shrine was merged.

In {{jalink|1956|Q5221}} (Shōwa 31), it was designated a {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592|Ginpeisha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

=== Main enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Himusubi Ōkami}}

=== Merged deities {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}} ===
* {{jalink|Kamado-gami|Q249178|Okutsuhiko Ōkami}}
* {{jalink|Kamado-gami|Q249178|Okutsuhime Ōkami}}
* Kushimiké Ōkami
* Hayatamao no Kami
* Yamikiri no Kami
* {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu Ōmikami}}
* {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōanamuchi no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukuhikona no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Takehayasusanoo no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Takeminakata|Q9150391|Takeminakata no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Yasakatome no Kami|Q9150392}}
* {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Kikurihime|Q11619891}}

=== Subsidiary shrines outside the precincts ===
* {{jalink|Shōkonsha|Q11497810}}
: Located on the former site of Kumano Shrine (Machikata, merged in 1913) {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}

=== {{jalink|Otabisho|Q11489117}} ===
* There are two locations: Machikata (former site of Kumano Shrine in Machikata, merged in 1913) and Bokata (former site of Kumano Shrine in Bokata, merged in 1907) {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

=== Natural monument ===
* The Great Cedar of Nyūkawa Shrine (designated natural monument of Takayama City) <ref>{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=https://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000994/1004127.html |title=List of Natural Monuments |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2025-05-02}}</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Nyuukawa Shrine (Uchi district)'''|丹生川神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{Shinto shrine
|Name        = Nyūkawa Shrine
|Image       = {{Image wanted|cat=Takayama City}}
|Location    = {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Takayama City|Q622594}} 2373 Bokata, Nyūkawa-chō
|Coordinates = {{Coord|36|9|59.3|N|137|19|2.2|E|scale:10000_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}}
|Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Himusubi Ōkami}}
|Shrine rank = {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592|Ginpeisha}}
|Established = Unknown
|Main hall   = 
|Alternate name = 
|Pilgrimage sites = 
|Annual festival = 
|Rituals     = 
}}
**Nyūkawa Shrine** (にゅうかわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Nyūkawa-chō (formerly {{jalink|Ōno District|Q31671}}{{jalink|Nyūkawa Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q7071339|Nyūkawa Village}}), {{jalink|Takayama City|Q622594}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}.

The shrine’s name is derived from {{jalink|Nyūkawa Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q7071339|Nyūkawa Village}} in {{jalink|Ōno District|Q31671}} and has no direct relation to {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}’s {{jalink|Niukawakami Shrine|Q246370}}, {{jalink|Niukawakami Shrine Upper Shrine|Q10877366}}, or {{jalink|Niukawakami Shrine Lower Shrine|Q10877367}}.

=== Overview ===
Originally, it was Yakama Shrine, enshrined in the Bokata district of Nyūkawa Village. During the shrine consolidation policy ({{jalink|Shinto shrine consolidation|Q11590700}}) in the late Meiji period, several shrines within the village were merged, and it was renamed **Nyūkawa Shrine**.

The date of establishment is unknown. In the past, it was known as “Yasō no Mori” and “Hachimen Aragū-gū” {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}}.

In {{jalink|1868|Q7717}} (Meiji 1), it was renamed Yakama Shrine {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}}. In {{jalink|1871|Q7746}} (Meiji 4), it became a village shrine.

In {{jalink|1907|Q2048}} (Meiji 40), Kumano Shrine and Kifune Shrine were merged into it. At the same time, an application was submitted to Gifu Prefecture to change the name from Yakama Shrine to Nyūkawa Shrine, which was approved the following year {{Sfn|Nyūkawa Village History|2000|p=436}}. In {{jalink|1913|Q2080}} (Taishō 2), Kumano Shrine was merged. In {{jalink|1914|Q2083}} (Taishō 3), it was officially renamed **Nyūkawa Shrine** {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

Thereafter, through the early Shōwa period, shrines within Nyūkawa Village (including Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Suwa Shrine, and Bijoshima Shrine) were successively merged {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}. As repeated mergers made the precincts cramped, the grounds were expanded and improved in {{jalink|1952|Q5272}} (Shōwa 27), and a new main hall and other buildings were constructed {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}. In the same year, Hakusan Shrine was merged.

In {{jalink|1956|Q5221}} (Shōwa 31), it was designated a {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592|Ginpeisha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

=== Main enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Himusubi Ōkami}}

=== Merged deities {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}} ===
* {{jalink|Kamado-gami|Q249178|Okutsuhiko Ōkami}}
* {{jalink|Kamado-gami|Q249178|Okutsuhime Ōkami}}
* Kushimiké Ōkami
* Hayatamao no Kami
* Yamikiri no Kami
* {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu Ōmikami}}
* {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōanamuchi no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukuhikona no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Takehayasusanoo no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Takeminakata|Q9150391|Takeminakata no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Yasakatome no Kami|Q9150392}}
* {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Kikurihime|Q11619891}}

=== Subsidiary shrines outside the precincts ===
* {{jalink|Shōkonsha|Q11497810}}
: Located on the former site of Kumano Shrine (Machikata, merged in 1913) {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}

=== {{jalink|Otabisho|Q11489117}} ===
* There are two locations: Machikata (former site of Kumano Shrine in Machikata, merged in 1913) and Bokata (former site of Kumano Shrine in Bokata, merged in 1907) {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

=== Natural monument ===
* The Great Cedar of Nyūkawa Shrine (designated natural monument of Takayama City) <ref>{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=https://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000994/1004127.html |title=List of Natural Monuments |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2025-05-02}}</ref>

== Japanese content ==
{{Shinto shrine
|Name        = Nyūkawa Shrine
|Image       = {{Image wanted|cat=Takayama City}}
|Location    = {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Takayama City|Q622594}} 2373 Bokata, Nyūkawa-chō
|Coordinates = {{Coord|36|9|59.3|N|137|19|2.2|E|scale:10000_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}}
|Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Himusubi Ōkami}}
|Shrine rank = {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592|Ginpeisha}}
|Established = Unknown
|Main hall   = 
|Alternate name = 
|Pilgrimage sites = 
|Annual festival = 
|Rituals     = 
}}
**Nyūkawa Shrine** (にゅうかわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Nyūkawa-chō (formerly {{jalink|Ōno District|Q31671}}{{jalink|Nyūkawa Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q7071339|Nyūkawa Village}}), {{jalink|Takayama City|Q622594}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}.

The shrine’s name is derived from {{jalink|Nyūkawa Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q7071339|Nyūkawa Village}} in {{jalink|Ōno District|Q31671}} and has no direct relation to {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}’s {{jalink|Niukawakami Shrine|Q246370}}, {{jalink|Niukawakami Shrine Upper Shrine|Q10877366}}, or {{jalink|Niukawakami Shrine Lower Shrine|Q10877367}}.

=== Overview ===
Originally, it was Yakama Shrine, enshrined in the Bokata district of Nyūkawa Village. During the shrine consolidation policy ({{jalink|Shinto shrine consolidation|Q11590700}}) in the late Meiji period, several shrines within the village were merged, and it was renamed **Nyūkawa Shrine**.

The date of establishment is unknown. In the past, it was known as “Yasō no Mori” and “Hachimen Aragū-gū” {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}}.

In {{jalink|1868|Q7717}} (Meiji 1), it was renamed Yakama Shrine {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}}. In {{jalink|1871|Q7746}} (Meiji 4), it became a village shrine.

In {{jalink|1907|Q2048}} (Meiji 40), Kumano Shrine and Kifune Shrine were merged into it. At the same time, an application was submitted to Gifu Prefecture to change the name from Yakama Shrine to Nyūkawa Shrine, which was approved the following year {{Sfn|Nyūkawa Village History|2000|p=436}}. In {{jalink|1913|Q2080}} (Taishō 2), Kumano Shrine was merged. In {{jalink|1914|Q2083}} (Taishō 3), it was officially renamed **Nyūkawa Shrine** {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

Thereafter, through the early Shōwa period, shrines within Nyūkawa Village (including Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Suwa Shrine, and Bijoshima Shrine) were successively merged {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}. As repeated mergers made the precincts cramped, the grounds were expanded and improved in {{jalink|1952|Q5272}} (Shōwa 27), and a new main hall and other buildings were constructed {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}. In the same year, Hakusan Shrine was merged.

In {{jalink|1956|Q5221}} (Shōwa 31), it was designated a {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592|Ginpeisha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

=== Main enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Himusubi Ōkami}}

=== Merged deities {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}} ===
* {{jalink|Kamado-gami|Q249178|Okutsuhiko Ōkami}}
* {{jalink|Kamado-gami|Q249178|Okutsuhime Ōkami}}
* Kushimiké Ōkami
* Hayatamao no Kami
* Yamikiri no Kami
* {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu Ōmikami}}
* {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōanamuchi no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukuhikona no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Takehayasusanoo no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Takeminakata|Q9150391|Takeminakata no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Yasakatome no Kami|Q9150392}}
* {{jalink|Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|Ichikishimahime no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami no Kami}}
* {{jalink|Kikurihime|Q11619891}}

=== Subsidiary shrines outside the precincts ===
* {{jalink|Shōkonsha|Q11497810}}
: Located on the former site of Kumano Shrine (Machikata, merged in 1913) {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}

=== {{jalink|Otabisho|Q11489117}} ===
* There are two locations: Machikata (former site of Kumano Shrine in Machikata, merged in 1913) and Bokata (former site of Kumano Shrine in Bokata, merged in 1907) {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

=== Natural monument ===
* The Great Cedar of Nyūkawa Shrine (designated natural monument of Takayama City) <ref>{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=https://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000994/1004127.html |title=List of Natural Monuments |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2025-05-02}}</ref>

== Japanese content ==
{{Shinto shrine
|Name        = Nyūkawa Shrine
|Image       = {{Image wanted|cat=Takayama City}}
|Location    = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takayama City|Q622594|WD=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}} 2373 Bokata, Nyūkawa-chō
|Coordinates = {{Coord|36|9|59.3|N|137|19|2.2|E|scale:10000_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}}
|Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Himusubi Ōkami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}

**Nyūkawa Shrine** (にゅうかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Nyūkawa-chō (formerly {{ill|lt=Ōno District|Q31671|WD=Q31671|1=Ōno District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Nyūkawa Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q7071339|WD=Q7071339|lt=Nyūkawa Village|1=Nyūkawa, Gifu}}), {{ill|lt=Takayama City|Q622594|WD=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}.

The shrine’s name is derived from {{ill|lt=Nyūkawa Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q7071339|WD=Q7071339|lt=Nyūkawa Village|1=Nyūkawa, Gifu}} in {{ill|lt=Ōno District|Q31671|WD=Q31671|1=Ōno District, Gifu}} and has no direct relation to {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}’s {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine|Q246370|WD=Q246370|1=Niukawakami Shrine (Q246370)}}, {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine Upper Shrine|Q10877366|WD=Q10877366|1=Niukawakami Upper Shrine}}, or {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine Lower Shrine|Q10877367|WD=Q10877367|1=Niukawakami Lower Shrine}}.

=== Overview ===
Originally, it was Yakama Shrine, enshrined in the Bokata district of Nyūkawa Village. During the shrine consolidation policy ({{ill|lt=Shinto shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}) in the late Meiji period, several shrines within the village were merged, and it was renamed **Nyūkawa Shrine**.

The date of establishment is unknown. In the past, it was known as “Yasō no Mori” and “Hachimen Aragū-gū” {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}}.

In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} (Meiji 1), it was renamed Yakama Shrine {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}}. In {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} (Meiji 4), it became a village shrine.

In {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), Kumano Shrine and Kifune Shrine were merged into it. At the same time, an application was submitted to Gifu Prefecture to change the name from Yakama Shrine to Nyūkawa Shrine, which was approved the following year {{Sfn|Nyūkawa Village History|2000|p=436}}. In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (Taishō 2), Kumano Shrine was merged. In {{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083|1=1914}} (Taishō 3), it was officially renamed **Nyūkawa Shrine** {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

Thereafter, through the early Shōwa period, shrines within Nyūkawa Village (including Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Suwa Shrine, and Bijoshima Shrine) were successively merged {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}. As repeated mergers made the precincts cramped, the grounds were expanded and improved in {{ill|lt=1952|Q5272|WD=Q5272|1=1952}} (Shōwa 27), and a new main hall and other buildings were constructed {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}. In the same year, Hakusan Shrine was merged.

In {{ill|lt=1956|Q5221|WD=Q5221|1=1956}} (Shōwa 31), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

=== Subsidiary shrines outside the precincts ===
* {{ill|lt=Shōkonsha|Q11497810|WD=Q11497810|1=Shōkonsha}}
: Located on the former site of Kumano Shrine (Machikata, merged in 1913) {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}

=== {{ill|lt=Otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} ===
* There are two locations: Machikata (former site of Kumano Shrine in Machikata, merged in 1913) and Bokata (former site of Kumano Shrine in Bokata, merged in 1907) {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

=== Natural monument ===
* The Great Cedar of Nyūkawa Shrine (designated natural monument of Takayama City) <ref>{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=https://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000994/1004127.html |title=List of Natural Monuments |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2025-05-02}}</ref>

== Japanese content ==
{{Shinto shrine
|Name        = Nyūkawa Shrine
|Image       = {{Image wanted|cat=Takayama City}}

|Coordinates = {{Coord|36|9|59.3|N|137|19|2.2|E|scale:10000_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}}
|Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Himusubi Ōkami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}

**Nyūkawa Shrine** (にゅうかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Nyūkawa-chō (formerly {{ill|lt=Ōno District|Q31671|WD=Q31671|1=Ōno District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Nyūkawa Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q7071339|WD=Q7071339|lt=Nyūkawa Village|1=Nyūkawa, Gifu}}), {{ill|lt=Takayama City|Q622594|WD=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}.

The shrine’s name is derived from {{ill|lt=Nyūkawa Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q7071339|WD=Q7071339|lt=Nyūkawa Village|1=Nyūkawa, Gifu}} in {{ill|lt=Ōno District|Q31671|WD=Q31671|1=Ōno District, Gifu}} and has no direct relation to {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}’s {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine|Q246370|WD=Q246370|1=Niukawakami Shrine (Q246370)}}, {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine Upper Shrine|Q10877366|WD=Q10877366|1=Niukawakami Upper Shrine}}, or {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine Lower Shrine|Q10877367|WD=Q10877367|1=Niukawakami Lower Shrine}}.

=== Overview ===
Originally, it was Yakama Shrine, enshrined in the Bokata district of Nyūkawa Village. During the shrine consolidation policy ({{ill|lt=Shinto shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}) in the late Meiji period, several shrines within the village were merged, and it was renamed **Nyūkawa Shrine**.

The date of establishment is unknown. In the past, it was known as “Yasō no Mori” and “Hachimen Aragū-gū” {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}}.

In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} (Meiji 1), it was renamed Yakama Shrine {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}}. In {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} (Meiji 4), it became a village shrine.

In {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), Kumano Shrine and Kifune Shrine were merged into it. At the same time, an application was submitted to Gifu Prefecture to change the name from Yakama Shrine to Nyūkawa Shrine, which was approved the following year {{Sfn|Nyūkawa Village History|2000|p=436}}. In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (Taishō 2), Kumano Shrine was merged. In {{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083|1=1914}} (Taishō 3), it was officially renamed **Nyūkawa Shrine** {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

Thereafter, through the early Shōwa period, shrines within Nyūkawa Village (including Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Suwa Shrine, and Bijoshima Shrine) were successively merged {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}. As repeated mergers made the precincts cramped, the grounds were expanded and improved in {{ill|lt=1952|Q5272|WD=Q5272|1=1952}} (Shōwa 27), and a new main hall and other buildings were constructed {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}. In the same year, Hakusan Shrine was merged.

In {{ill|lt=1956|Q5221|WD=Q5221|1=1956}} (Shōwa 31), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

=== Subsidiary shrines outside the precincts ===
* {{ill|lt=Shōkonsha|Q11497810|WD=Q11497810|1=Shōkonsha}}
: Located on the former site of Kumano Shrine (Machikata, merged in 1913) {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}

=== {{ill|lt=Otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} ===
* There are two locations: Machikata (former site of Kumano Shrine in Machikata, merged in 1913) and Bokata (former site of Kumano Shrine in Bokata, merged in 1907) {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

=== Natural monument ===
* The Great Cedar of Nyūkawa Shrine (designated natural monument of Takayama City) <ref>{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=https://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000994/1004127.html |title=List of Natural Monuments |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2025-05-02}}</ref>

== Japanese content ==
{{Shinto shrine
|Name        = Nyūkawa Shrine
|Image       = {{Image wanted|cat=Takayama City}}
|Location    = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takayama City|Q622594|WD=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}} 2373 Bokata, Nyūkawa-chō
|Coordinates = {{Coord|36|9|59.3|N|137|19|2.2|E|scale:10000_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}}
|Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Himusubi Ōkami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}
|Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}}

**Nyūkawa Shrine** (にゅうかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Nyūkawa-chō (formerly {{ill|lt=Ōno District|Q31671|WD=Q31671|1=Ōno District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Nyūkawa Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q7071339|WD=Q7071339|lt=Nyūkawa Village|1=Nyūkawa, Gifu}}), {{ill|lt=Takayama City|Q622594|WD=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}.

The shrine’s name is derived from {{ill|lt=Nyūkawa Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q7071339|WD=Q7071339|lt=Nyūkawa Village|1=Nyūkawa, Gifu}} in {{ill|lt=Ōno District|Q31671|WD=Q31671|1=Ōno District, Gifu}} and has no direct relation to {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}’s {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine|Q246370|WD=Q246370|1=Niukawakami Shrine (Q246370)}}, {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine Upper Shrine|Q10877366|WD=Q10877366|1=Niukawakami Upper Shrine}}, or {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine Lower Shrine|Q10877367|WD=Q10877367|1=Niukawakami Lower Shrine}}.

=== Overview ===
Originally, it was Yakama Shrine, enshrined in the Bokata district of Nyūkawa Village. During the shrine consolidation policy ({{ill|lt=Shinto shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}) in the late Meiji period, several shrines within the village were merged, and it was renamed **Nyūkawa Shrine**.

The date of establishment is unknown. In the past, it was known as “Yasō no Mori” and “Hachimen Aragū-gū” {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}}.

In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} (Meiji 1), it was renamed Yakama Shrine {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}}. In {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} (Meiji 4), it became a village shrine.

In {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), Kumano Shrine and Kifune Shrine were merged into it. At the same time, an application was submitted to Gifu Prefecture to change the name from Yakama Shrine to Nyūkawa Shrine, which was approved the following year {{Sfn|Nyūkawa Village History|2000|p=436}}. In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (Taishō 2), Kumano Shrine was merged. In {{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083|1=1914}} (Taishō 3), it was officially renamed **Nyūkawa Shrine** {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

Thereafter, through the early Shōwa period, shrines within Nyūkawa Village (including Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Suwa Shrine, and Bijoshima Shrine) were successively merged {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}. As repeated mergers made the precincts cramped, the grounds were expanded and improved in {{ill|lt=1952|Q5272|WD=Q5272|1=1952}} (Shōwa 27), and a new main hall and other buildings were constructed {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}. In the same year, Hakusan Shrine was merged.

In {{ill|lt=1956|Q5221|WD=Q5221|1=1956}} (Shōwa 31), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

=== {{ill|lt=Otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} ===
* There are two locations: Machikata (former site of Kumano Shrine in Machikata, merged in 1913) and Bokata (former site of Kumano Shrine in Bokata, merged in 1907) {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

{{nihongo|'''Nyuukawa Shrine (Uchi district)'''|丹生川神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{Shinto shrine
|Name        = Nyūkawa Shrine
|Image       = {{Image wanted|cat=Takayama City}}
|Location    = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takayama City|Q622594|WD=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}} 2373 Bokata, Nyūkawa-chō
|Coordinates = {{Coord|36|9|59.3|N|137|19|2.2|E|scale:10000_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}}
|Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Himusubi Ōkami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}
|Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}}
|Established = Unknown

**Nyūkawa Shrine** (にゅうかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Nyūkawa-chō (formerly {{ill|lt=Ōno District|Q31671|WD=Q31671|1=Ōno District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Nyūkawa Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q7071339|WD=Q7071339|lt=Nyūkawa Village|1=Nyūkawa, Gifu}}), {{ill|lt=Takayama City|Q622594|WD=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}.

The shrine’s name is derived from {{ill|lt=Nyūkawa Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q7071339|WD=Q7071339|lt=Nyūkawa Village|1=Nyūkawa, Gifu}} in {{ill|lt=Ōno District|Q31671|WD=Q31671|1=Ōno District, Gifu}} and has no direct relation to {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}’s {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine|Q246370|WD=Q246370|1=Niukawakami Shrine (Q246370)}}, {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine Upper Shrine|Q10877366|WD=Q10877366|1=Niukawakami Upper Shrine}}, or {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine Lower Shrine|Q10877367|WD=Q10877367|1=Niukawakami Lower Shrine}}.

=== Overview ===
Originally, it was Yakama Shrine, enshrined in the Bokata district of Nyūkawa Village. During the shrine consolidation policy ({{ill|lt=Shinto shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}) in the late Meiji period, several shrines within the village were merged, and it was renamed **Nyūkawa Shrine**.

The date of establishment is unknown. In the past, it was known as “Yasō no Mori” and “Hachimen Aragū-gū” {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}}.

In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} (Meiji 1), it was renamed Yakama Shrine {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}}. In {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} (Meiji 4), it became a village shrine.

In {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), Kumano Shrine and Kifune Shrine were merged into it. At the same time, an application was submitted to Gifu Prefecture to change the name from Yakama Shrine to Nyūkawa Shrine, which was approved the following year {{Sfn|Nyūkawa Village History|2000|p=436}}. In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (Taishō 2), Kumano Shrine was merged. In {{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083|1=1914}} (Taishō 3), it was officially renamed **Nyūkawa Shrine** {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

Thereafter, through the early Shōwa period, shrines within Nyūkawa Village (including Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Suwa Shrine, and Bijoshima Shrine) were successively merged {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}. As repeated mergers made the precincts cramped, the grounds were expanded and improved in {{ill|lt=1952|Q5272|WD=Q5272|1=1952}} (Shōwa 27), and a new main hall and other buildings were constructed {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}. In the same year, Hakusan Shrine was merged.

In {{ill|lt=1956|Q5221|WD=Q5221|1=1956}} (Shōwa 31), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

=== {{ill|lt=Otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} ===
* There are two locations: Machikata (former site of Kumano Shrine in Machikata, merged in 1913) and Bokata (former site of Kumano Shrine in Bokata, merged in 1907) {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

{{nihongo|'''Nyuukawa Shrine (Uchi district)'''|丹生川神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{Shinto shrine
|Name        = Nyūkawa Shrine
|Image       = {{Image wanted|cat=Takayama City}}
|Location    = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takayama City|Q622594|WD=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}} 2373 Bokata, Nyūkawa-chō
|Coordinates = {{Coord|36|9|59.3|N|137|19|2.2|E|scale:10000_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}}
|Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Himusubi Ōkami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}
|Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}}
|Established = Unknown
|Main hall   =

**Nyūkawa Shrine** (にゅうかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Nyūkawa-chō (formerly {{ill|lt=Ōno District|Q31671|WD=Q31671|1=Ōno District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Nyūkawa Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q7071339|WD=Q7071339|lt=Nyūkawa Village|1=Nyūkawa, Gifu}}), {{ill|lt=Takayama City|Q622594|WD=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}.

The shrine’s name is derived from {{ill|lt=Nyūkawa Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q7071339|WD=Q7071339|lt=Nyūkawa Village|1=Nyūkawa, Gifu}} in {{ill|lt=Ōno District|Q31671|WD=Q31671|1=Ōno District, Gifu}} and has no direct relation to {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}’s {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine|Q246370|WD=Q246370|1=Niukawakami Shrine (Q246370)}}, {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine Upper Shrine|Q10877366|WD=Q10877366|1=Niukawakami Upper Shrine}}, or {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine Lower Shrine|Q10877367|WD=Q10877367|1=Niukawakami Lower Shrine}}.

=== Overview ===
Originally, it was Yakama Shrine, enshrined in the Bokata district of Nyūkawa Village. During the shrine consolidation policy ({{ill|lt=Shinto shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}) in the late Meiji period, several shrines within the village were merged, and it was renamed **Nyūkawa Shrine**.

The date of establishment is unknown. In the past, it was known as “Yasō no Mori” and “Hachimen Aragū-gū” {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}}.

In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} (Meiji 1), it was renamed Yakama Shrine {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}}. In {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} (Meiji 4), it became a village shrine.

In {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), Kumano Shrine and Kifune Shrine were merged into it. At the same time, an application was submitted to Gifu Prefecture to change the name from Yakama Shrine to Nyūkawa Shrine, which was approved the following year {{Sfn|Nyūkawa Village History|2000|p=436}}. In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (Taishō 2), Kumano Shrine was merged. In {{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083|1=1914}} (Taishō 3), it was officially renamed **Nyūkawa Shrine** {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

Thereafter, through the early Shōwa period, shrines within Nyūkawa Village (including Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Suwa Shrine, and Bijoshima Shrine) were successively merged {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}. As repeated mergers made the precincts cramped, the grounds were expanded and improved in {{ill|lt=1952|Q5272|WD=Q5272|1=1952}} (Shōwa 27), and a new main hall and other buildings were constructed {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}. In the same year, Hakusan Shrine was merged.

In {{ill|lt=1956|Q5221|WD=Q5221|1=1956}} (Shōwa 31), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

=== {{ill|lt=Otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} ===
* There are two locations: Machikata (former site of Kumano Shrine in Machikata, merged in 1913) and Bokata (former site of Kumano Shrine in Bokata, merged in 1907) {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

{{nihongo|'''Nyuukawa Shrine (Uchi district)'''|丹生川神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

**Nyūkawa Shrine** (にゅうかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Nyūkawa-chō (formerly {{ill|lt=Ōno District|Q31671|WD=Q31671|1=Ōno District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Nyūkawa Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q7071339|WD=Q7071339|lt=Nyūkawa Village|1=Nyūkawa, Gifu}}), {{ill|lt=Takayama City|Q622594|WD=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}.

The shrine’s name is derived from {{ill|lt=Nyūkawa Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q7071339|WD=Q7071339|lt=Nyūkawa Village|1=Nyūkawa, Gifu}} in {{ill|lt=Ōno District|Q31671|WD=Q31671|1=Ōno District, Gifu}} and has no direct relation to {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}’s {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine|Q246370|WD=Q246370|1=Niukawakami Shrine (Q246370)}}, {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine Upper Shrine|Q10877366|WD=Q10877366|1=Niukawakami Upper Shrine}}, or {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine Lower Shrine|Q10877367|WD=Q10877367|1=Niukawakami Lower Shrine}}.

=== Overview ===
Originally, it was Yakama Shrine, enshrined in the Bokata district of Nyūkawa Village. During the shrine consolidation policy ({{ill|lt=Shinto shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}) in the late Meiji period, several shrines within the village were merged, and it was renamed **Nyūkawa Shrine**.

The date of establishment is unknown. In the past, it was known as “Yasō no Mori” and “Hachimen Aragū-gū” {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}}.

In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} (Meiji 1), it was renamed Yakama Shrine {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}}. In {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} (Meiji 4), it became a village shrine.

In {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), Kumano Shrine and Kifune Shrine were merged into it. At the same time, an application was submitted to Gifu Prefecture to change the name from Yakama Shrine to Nyūkawa Shrine, which was approved the following year {{Sfn|Nyūkawa Village History|2000|p=436}}. In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (Taishō 2), Kumano Shrine was merged. In {{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083|1=1914}} (Taishō 3), it was officially renamed **Nyūkawa Shrine** {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

Thereafter, through the early Shōwa period, shrines within Nyūkawa Village (including Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Suwa Shrine, and Bijoshima Shrine) were successively merged {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}. As repeated mergers made the precincts cramped, the grounds were expanded and improved in {{ill|lt=1952|Q5272|WD=Q5272|1=1952}} (Shōwa 27), and a new main hall and other buildings were constructed {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}. In the same year, Hakusan Shrine was merged.

In {{ill|lt=1956|Q5221|WD=Q5221|1=1956}} (Shōwa 31), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

{{nihongo|'''Nyuukawa Shrine (Uchi district)'''|丹生川神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

**Nyūkawa Shrine** (にゅうかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Nyūkawa-chō (formerly {{ill|lt=Ōno District|Q31671|WD=Q31671|1=Ōno District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Nyūkawa Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q7071339|WD=Q7071339|lt=Nyūkawa Village|1=Nyūkawa, Gifu}}), {{ill|lt=Takayama City|Q622594|WD=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}.

The shrine’s name is derived from {{ill|lt=Nyūkawa Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q7071339|WD=Q7071339|lt=Nyūkawa Village|1=Nyūkawa, Gifu}} in {{ill|lt=Ōno District|Q31671|WD=Q31671|1=Ōno District, Gifu}} and has no direct relation to {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}’s {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine|Q246370|WD=Q246370|1=Niukawakami Shrine (Q246370)}}, {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine Upper Shrine|Q10877366|WD=Q10877366|1=Niukawakami Upper Shrine}}, or {{ill|lt=Niukawakami Shrine Lower Shrine|Q10877367|WD=Q10877367|1=Niukawakami Lower Shrine}}.

=== Overview ===
Originally, it was Yakama Shrine, enshrined in the Bokata district of Nyūkawa Village. During the shrine consolidation policy ({{ill|lt=Shinto shrine consolidation|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}) in the late Meiji period, several shrines within the village were merged, and it was renamed **Nyūkawa Shrine**.

The date of establishment is unknown. In the past, it was known as “Yasō no Mori” and “Hachimen Aragū-gū” {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}}.

In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} (Meiji 1), it was renamed Yakama Shrine {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=43}}. In {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} (Meiji 4), it became a village shrine.

In {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), Kumano Shrine and Kifune Shrine were merged into it. At the same time, an application was submitted to Gifu Prefecture to change the name from Yakama Shrine to Nyūkawa Shrine, which was approved the following year {{Sfn|Nyūkawa Village History|2000|p=436}}. In {{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080|1=1913}} (Taishō 2), Kumano Shrine was merged. In {{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083|1=1914}} (Taishō 3), it was officially renamed **Nyūkawa Shrine** {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

Thereafter, through the early Shōwa period, shrines within Nyūkawa Village (including Shinmei Shrine, Kumano Shrine, Suwa Shrine, and Bijoshima Shrine) were successively merged {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}. As repeated mergers made the precincts cramped, the grounds were expanded and improved in {{ill|lt=1952|Q5272|WD=Q5272|1=1952}} (Shōwa 27), and a new main hall and other buildings were constructed {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}. In the same year, Hakusan Shrine was merged.

In {{ill|lt=1956|Q5221|WD=Q5221|1=1956}} (Shōwa 31), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{Sfn|Tsuchida|1987|p=44}}.

=== {{ill|lt=Otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}} ===

{{nihongo|'''Nyuukawa Shrine (Uchi district)'''|丹生川神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].
